data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Apple's website champions clean, minimal design which enhances perceived usability.
data.technical_spec: Strategic use of white space, high-quality images, and concise text.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: The site is designed to minimize cognitive load, ensuring users make decisions quickly and easily.
data.technical_spec: Simple navigation menus with limited options to reduce decision time.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Interactive elements are strategically placed to ensure ease of access and interaction.
data.technical_spec: Large clickable buttons and links aim for maximum target size proximity.

data.title: Law of Prägnanz
data.description: The design layout is simple and clear, encouraging users to perceive comprehensible patterns.
data.technical_spec: Use of grid systems and modular design to guide user focus effectively.

data.title: Zeigarnik Effect
data.description: Engages users with visually interruptive content to increase memory retention and interaction.
data.technical_spec: Interactive calls-to-action and animation cues promote user engagement.
